ch8_start.text: You hear an incessant scratching and squealing coming from the ventilation shafts. Life support warnings are flashing across the corridor monitors. The noise is concentrated near the Xenobiology Lab.

ch8_lab_enter.text: The lab is a nightmare. Hundreds of rats, their bodies mutated by the glowing toxin, are tearing through the life support filters. In the center of the room, Victor, the senior xenobiologist, is writing frantically on the glass walls. His eyes are milky white, and black veins cover his neck.
ch8_lab_enter.speaker: Victor

ch8_victor_reveal.text: Victor turns slowly. 'Magnificent, aren't they?' he whispers. 'They adapt so quickly. The organism is breeding in them. It's rewriting their neural pathways. I thought... I thought it could fix my dementia. Restore my mind.'
ch8_victor_reveal.speaker: Victor

ch8_victor_truth.text: 'I couldn't remember my wife's face anymore!' he cries, tearing at his hair. 'The mud... it gave me memories back. But they aren't mine. They belong to the swarm now. The swarm remembers everything.' He suddenly lunges at the containment glass, shattering it.

choices.text: Run out of the lab and seal it.
Listen to the memories of the swarm before fleeing.

ch8_listen_swarm.text: You close your eyes. The squealing of the rats becomes a choir of whispers. You see visions of ancient, dark waters and hunger. You understand the organism's simple desire: to consume and record all life. The knowledge burns your mind, but you understand your enemy.
